game_name: Might and Magic III: Isles of Terra

design_philosophy.core_loop: First-person turn-based dungeon exploration with party-based combat, puzzle-solving, and character progression across interconnected island worlds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health, mana, equipment) balanced against exploration of dangerous dungeons and confrontation with increasingly powerful adversaries
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a party of adventurers across exotic island worlds, uncovering mysteries of ancient civilizations while battling supernatural threats and pursuing an ongoing cosmic conflict
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to turn-based tactical combat with clear distance-based ranged mechanics; features persistent world state (defeated enemies stay dead) and player agency in saving; embraces complexity with automapping and graphical UI improvements while maintaining old-school dungeon crawling sensibilities

difficulty.checkpointing: Liberal save feature allowing game state preservation at almost any time except Arena and Castle Greywind Dungeon; departed from prior games' inn-based saving
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High player agency: permadeath for individual party members possible; enemy encounters visible at distance allowing tactical avoidance; ranged combat allows varied tactical approaches; save system prevents progress loss but not character death

progression.upgrade_system: Character leveling and equipment progression implied; spell selection system from lists rather than numeric codes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression through pursuit of Sheltem and Corak across multiple island nacelles; discovery of Maze from Hell and Pyramids of the Ancients unlock advanced content and 'Ultimate Adventurers' title
replayability_hooks: Multiple starting character options (canon party: Sir Caneghem, Crag Hack, Maximus, Resurrectra, Dark Shade, Kastore, Robert the Wise, Tolberti)
Persistent world state encourages exploration planning
Large fantasy world with multiple puzzle solutions implied

world_building_techniques: Journal entries from Corak establishing lore of Ancients, Elemental Lords, and Forces of the Dome
Environmental discovery: revelation that the underwater seedship 'Lincoln' is an ancient colonization vessel
Ongoing conflict narrative spanning multiple games: continuation of Sheltem pursuit arc from MM2 into MM4
Dialogue and text describing nacelle worlds being destroyed by Sheltem
Non-player character cameos: party members appear as powerful NPCs in MM7, bridging narrative gaps

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating supernatural and cosmic adversaries; Sheltem as recurring antagonist; Maze from Hell serves as gauntlet challenge granting titular status
notable_bosses: Sheltem (recurring antagonist, escapes via underwater ship)
The Guardians (mysterious opposing forces in cosmic conflict)
Maze from Hell defenses (gauntlet challenge awarding 'Ultimate Adventurers' title)

faction_indicators: Corak's allegiance (mysterious but allied with party)
danger_signals: Nacelles being launched into suns (mass destruction events)
Sheltem's active machinations across multiple worlds
Castle Greywind Dungeon (unsave location indicating high-danger area)
environmental_storytelling: Island nacelle worlds with distinct characteristics
Underwater seedship discovery revealing ancient colonization history
Multiple dungeons with escalating challenges reflecting narrative progression
Varied locales including Fountain Head starting town, Arena, Pyramids of the Ancients
